pages.title: Art | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Art Department at Sacramento State fosters expression through visual arts via courses in art education, art history, ceramics, drawing, new media art, painting, printmaking, and interdisciplinary sculpture.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Engage With the World of Art
contentBlocks: The Department of Art introduces students to the expressive world of the visual arts. Students are able to explore art through courses in art education, art history, ceramics, drawing, new media art, painting, printmaking, sculpture, and social practice.

The Department’s undergraduate program offers courses in four general areas Art History, Studio Arts Methods, and Art Studio, while the fourth prepares students to teach with a Master of Arts degree in Studio Art .
Thurs, Feb 12 - Thurs, Apr 30 Painting La Comuniversidad
Tues, Apr 28 - Thurs, May 14 Senior Show: Spring 2026
Art Department Kadema Hall, Room 185 Spring Hours, Mon – Fri, 8AM – 5PM with closures MW 8AM–9PM and closure F 3–5PM

pages.title: Undergraduate Advising Information for Art Students | Sacramento State
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Undergraduate Advising Information for Art Students
Preparing for Your First Advising Session
contentBlocks: Each undergraduate student who declares an Art major is automatically assigned to an Art Advisor in Art Education, Art History, or Studio Art. All Art Advisors are full-time faculty in the Art Department. Students can work with their assigned advisor or they can request a specific Art faculty member to be their advisor and this request will be reviewed by the Chair. Visit the "Meet Us" page for a list of all Art Advisors names, emails, and office hours.

Students are urged to meet with an Art faculty advisor as soon as possible after declaring Art as a major. All Art majors are strongly encouraged to meet with an Art faculty advisor at least once each academic year, usually a few weeks before enrolling in classes for the next term.

When students begin Upper Division coursework in their concentration, it is beneficial to meet again with an advisor to discuss appropriate substitutions, catalog rights, and elective selection. This will better prepare students for applying to graduate and ensure the student is able to complete all the major requirements before their projected graduation date.
When the time comes, students should meet with their advisor again before submitting their online graduation application to confirm the accuracy and completeness of the student’s major requirements.

All full-time Art faculty members advise students during regularly scheduled office hours or by prearranged appointments. Advisor names and contact information are posted on the "Meet Us" page and in the Art Office. The Art Office won’t make advisor appointments for students. Email the advisor to arrange an advising appointment if you can’t make it to their office hours.

Collect transcripts as evidence of college level art courses taken at other colleges. Or if records are current, this information can be reviewed on MySacState Student Center. Students should also review the Art major requirements that apply to their Catalog year.

Read information about current lower division transfer agreements for Sacramento State with other CSU campuses and California community colleges on Assist.org . This resource allows advisors to quickly review course articulation agreements. Art courses taken at other institutions may be used toward the major with Art Advisor approval. If necessary, documentation of these courses (catalog descriptions, syllabi, assignments, etc.) may be requested by the Art Advisor. A portfolio of work done in other college art classes may be required to determine equivalency.
Preparing for Your First Advising Session

pages.title: MFA in Studio Art | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Master of Fine Arts in Studio Art degree is a two-year, 60-unit, multi-disciplinary and interdisciplinary program in studio art with an emphasis on the individual artist, holistically preparing for a professional career in the arts.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
contentBlocks: Fall 2026 Application Deadline is passed.
Check back for our next application period. Application Deadline: Cal State Apply

The Master of Fine Arts in Studio Art degree is a two-year, 60-unit, multi-disciplinary and interdisciplinary program in studio art with an emphasis on the individual artist, holistically preparing for a professional career in the arts.

The Master of Fine Arts in Studio Art offers rigorous study in the discipline, fostering an environment for experimentation, discovery and mastery. The multi-disciplinary and interdisciplinary nature of the program allows artists to work and think across media. The Department of Art has facilities for ceramics, drawing, new media art, painting, printmaking, sculpture, and social practice . MFA students have access to all facilities, studios and shops and each graduate student has a private studio space.
Sacramento is a vibrant city with an active art community and many opportunities for community engagement. Our on-campus galleries hot exhibitions and visiting artists.

The members of the studio faculty are distinguished and active professionals with regional, national and international reputations. Students are guided by the studio faculty, with a studio advisor and the graduate coordinator to mentor them through the program. It is our mission to provide a positive and supportive graduate experience at Sacramento State.

The MFA curriculum includes studio courses, seminars, and courses in art history and in art theory and criticism, with elective opportunities to explore other academic interests. The central focus of the program is the development of bodies of works and the research that supports them. Between five and eight Graduate Students are accepted to the program each Fall. Each incoming cohort is distributed evenly across media. First and second year cohorts meet in seminar and studio courses to discuss their work from diverse perspectives.
For additional information about the MFA in Studio Art, please see the catalogue, course descriptions, Faculty Info, and Admission and Application Procedure in the links below.

Applying to graduate school at Sacramento State entails a simultaneous two part process, and all steps must be completed by the application deadlines. Follow this link to read more about the admission process for our Studio Art MFA.

The Department of Art facilities at Sacramento State are designed to support students’ needs in pursuing various disciplines in studio art. Graduate students have access to all Art Department facilities in consultation with faculty and staff. The facilities are located in:
Kadema Hall: Ceramics, printmaking, painting and drawing, new media art, and social practice spaces.
Mariposa Hall: New media art’s main computer media lab.
Art Studio Lab: Painting and drawing, sculpture, social practice, and graduate program studio spaces.
New Art Studio Lab Facility: including new graduate wing.

The Department of Art has several gallery spaces on campus. Robert Else and R.W. and Joyce Witt Gallery are located at Kadema Hall for student and professional exhibitions. Located in the main library is the University Library Gallery where national and international professional exhibits rotate throughout the year.

All MFA students will have full access to the graduate studios and be assigned a private studio space depending on media discipline. Proximity to labs, shops and equipment is considered when assigning studio spaces. A communal graduate space is located in the Art Sculpture Lab where the majority of graduates’ have their spaces.

All graduate students in the MFA program at Sacramento State must demonstrate graduate level proficiency in writing in accordance with University Graduate Studies standards. This writing proficiency is assessed through ART 206 Art Theory and Criticism, Graduate Writing Intensive (GWI) Course.

Degree completion occurs when the student has fulfilled all University and Departmental requirements of the MFA in Studio Art Degree. This is detailed in the Graduate Handbook that is the graduate students’ guide through the program from admissions to graduation.
The MFA program requires successful completion of all core and elective course work, successful completion of all semester reviews and Advancement to Candidacy solo exhibition review.

In the last term of the degree students will enroll in ART 500A and ART 500B Culminating Experience Exhibition and Thesis in which the final exhibition review and submission of written abstract (thesis) will be assessed by the Faculty.

Sacramento State has financial aid packages available and are recommended to apply for financial aid by priority date of March 2nd for Fall admissions, although applicants may apply at any time after priority date. The two-year MFA program is $4,518 per semester. On average, students can anticipate approximately $18,000 in total tuition costs. Out-of-State applicants can anticipate an additional $396 per unit for the first year or until residency is established. Tuition and fees are subject to annual precentage increases.
The MFA program also offers opportunities in teaching, art technician work and gallery operations in various forms including Federal Work Study.
Pay rates are per semester Federal work study $1,000-1,500 Instructional Student Assistant (ISA) $2,000-4,800 Graduate Assistant (GA) $2,000-3,300 Teaching Associate (TA) $3,200-4,500

Graduate Scholarships Annual student scholarships and awards range from $800 to $5,000; each has its own unique requirements.Art Department scholarships include the Jennette B. Yuppa Scholarship, Ruth Rippon Ceramics Scholarship, and the ACE Scholarship.
For a more information please visit the links below.

pages.title: Scholarships | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Department of Art sponsors over eight scholarships and awards programs for its students each year. Applications for these awards are accepted- and winners are announced- each Spring semester. Each semester, the Department hires Sacramento State students to as graders, studio assistants, and gallery monitors.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Scholarships on Sac State's Scholarship Portal
Awards Available in the Annual Student Award Show
R.W. and Joyce Witt Art History Competition
contentBlocks: The Department of Art sponsors several scholarships and awards programs for its students each year. Applications for these awards are accepted starting in October and winners are announced each Spring semester. To be eligible to win one of these scholarships, applicants must be active students and meet that scholarship's individual eligibility requirements during the semester the money will be awarded.
The deadline to apply for these scholarships is March 2, 2026 at 11:59 pm. Both the scholarship and award recipients will be announced at the Annual Awards Show in Spring 2026.
Apply now on Sac State's Scholarship Portal!

The Academic and Creative Excellence Award is a $1000 award to support an outstanding undergraduate or graduate Art major. Open to Sophomore, Junior, Senior, and Graduate students in good academic standing and an overall GPA of 2.0 for Undergrad and 3.0 for Grad.

The Jeannette B. Yuppa Scholarship is a $1000 award to support an outstanding undergraduate or graduate Art major who has chosen teaching at any level as a future goal. Two recipients are chosen a year.

The Marla Chicconi Memorial Endowed Art Scholarship is a $1500 award to support an outstanding undergraduate Art major with an interest in teaching. Open to Sophomore, Junior, and Senior students with a declared major in Art and an overall GPA of 2.0.

The Juanita and Jose Montoya Memorial Art Scholarship is a $1500 award to support an outstanding undergraduate Art major with an interest in teaching. Open to Junior and Senior students with a declared major in Art and an overall GPA of 2.7.
The ClayPeople Award is an award to support an undergrad or graduate Art student who is enrolled in a ceramics course in the year that they apply. A portfolio is required.

The Ruth Rippon Ceramic Scholarship is a $1000 award offered once a year to an outstanding student majoring in Studio Art, with an emphasis in ceramics, who shows promise in making ceramic art a career.
The Peggy Saunders Hall Memorial Scholarship is a $5,000 award to support an outstanding undergraduate Art student who has chosen teaching at any level as a future goal.

The Department of Art and the University Galleries hold their annual Awards Show each spring. The exhibition is juried by two guest jurors and features work by current art students. The Awards Show exhibition includes several award categories as well as merit awards. The competition is open to undergraduate and graduate students, as well as all matriculated and Open University students, enrolled at Sac State during the current academic year. Below are available monetary awards available to students.
The R. W. and Joyce Witt Scholarship Award in Studio Art for any Sacramento State student with a declared minor or major in Art or Photography. All media are eligible. Award amount varies per year.

The Frederick M. Peyser, Sr. Prize in Studio Art for any Sacramento State undergraduate. Only two-dimensional media (painting, photography, printmaking, mixed media, and digital, video and interactive works) are eligible. Award amount varies per year.
The Increase Robinson Memorial Fellowship Award is for classified graduate students in Studio Art. Entries may be in any medium. Each award is $1000. This award may be won only once.
The R. W. and Joyce Witt Art History Competition gives out two awards of $200 each. One prize will be awarded for each of the two following categories. Students can submit one entry to each category.
Category A: graduate or undergraduate art history research papers .
Category B: graduate or undergraduate art writing of any genre .

A faculty sponsor is required for each submission. Any applicant must be enrolled at Sacramento State in Spring 2026 and must have a declared major or minor in Art or Photography. Any graduate student must have classified status and must be enrolled in the Studio Art MFA program or in a special major MA program in art, art history, art education, or art administration.
Check back here in January 2026 for more information about our Spring 2026 competition!
Scholarships on Sac State's Scholarship Portal
Awards Available in the Annual Student Award Show
R.W. and Joyce Witt Art History Competition

pages.title: Employment Opportunities | Sacramento State
pages.description: Sac State Art Department Employment Opportunities.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
contentBlocks: The Department of Art anticipates a need for part-time instructors. Salary rate is variable depending upon qualifications and relevant experience.
Applications will remain open until filled.

Please visit our new Career site linked below for the position details, list of qualifications, and applicant instructions. To be considered to teach in 2025-2026 , please submit your materials, following the guidelines provided in the link. 3YR contract faculty need only submit if they are in the third year of a three-year contract.
Apply for the 2025/26 Art Department Lecturer Pool

The Art Department is now accepting applications for our part-time Art Model hiring pool . Modeling is in the nude and involves a variety of poses sustained over varying periods of time. Skills required include the ability to hold poses requested by the Art instructor over specified periods of time (short gestural or action poses, longer poses for more detailed studies of the figure, etc.). Apply today! Pool will open again soon!

The Art Department employs Instructional Student Assistants to provide support as studio assistants in the New Media and Printmaking labs, and as graders for large Art History lecture classes. Upper division and graduate students are encouraged to apply for Academic Student Employee positions. Hiring criteria vary with each type of job and is subject to funding.
All positions have been filled for the Fall 2025 semester, please check back in January for Spring hiring information.
Federal Work Study positions are also available to provide support in the Art Studio Lab and to work as an Inventory and Media Assistant. Students must be awarded Federal Work Study to be considered.

FWS Technician Assistants will help with the general class use of lab facilities and will function as directed in maintaining a clean and safe work environment in all areas operated by the Art department.
The FWS Inventory and Media Assistant will assist in the development and execution of a new inventory system and the set up of the new virtual reality lab.

pages.title: Art Clubs | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Art Department's variety of clubs connects students and artists to get involved in events and exhibitions regardless of discipline or media.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Sac State Chapter of the National Art Education Association
contentBlocks: The Art Department has a variety of clubs that students can get involved in, no matter what their focus is.

Joining an art club can help connect you to other student artists and artists in the greater Sacramento community, and is a great way to get involved in events and exhibitions and gain valuable new experiences.

The Art History Club at Sac State strives to build a community of artists, historians, educators, and students of all disciplines. We hope to create opportunities for students to have an outreach into the Sacramento art community through a variety of events such as films, second Saturday events and museum visits. Faculty advisor: Rachel Miller

The purpose of the Ceramic Guild is to increase interest in the ceramic department, raise money for new equipment, and gain new experiences. This is a club for both students and members of the Sacramento community. Faculty advisor: Scott Parady

Printmaking has a rich history of making art, information and revolutionary ideas available to the public. In the Printmaking Club, the aim is to revive the art and science of traditional printmaking within our campus community. Members are accepted from all academic disciplines. No printmaking experience required. Faculty advisor: Summer Ventis

The Sacramento State student chapter of NAEA is coming this Fall 2019. This is the world’s largest professional art education association with over 20,000 members across the country. Membership is open to art students, art museum educators, non-art educators, teaching artists, and studio artists. A membership would be to the state and national associations with no additional cost. Faculty advisor: Luis-Genaro Garcia

pages.title: Events in the Art Department | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Department of Art sponsors an array of free public events throughout the year. Enjoy the many exhibitions, lectures, and more on the Sacramento State campus.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
contentBlocks: The Department of Art sponsors a full array of free public events at Sacramento State each year including art exhibitions by faculty, students and visiting artists, visiting scholar lectures, the Annual Student Awards Show, the Art History Symposium and the end of year Art Ball.

All of the artistic media taught in the Department of Art are represented in the Robert Else Gallery and R.W. & Joyce Witt Gallery in Kadema Hall. Most of the shows are designed and installed by Art students. Students and faculty are frequently involved in the University Union Gallery and University Library Gallery exhibitions.
Admission to events sponsored by the Department of Art is free and open to the public.

R.W. & Joyce Witt Gallery proposals are accepted by the Gallery & Events Committee each spring for consideration for the following academic year. Refer to the Witt Gallery Exhibition Guide and to the Witt Gallery Application for details and deadlines. Witt Gallery exhibitors are responsible for funding, publicizing, installing and staffing the exhibitions.
Check back here in Spring 2026 for information on how to apply for the 2026/2027 academic year.

The Department of Art and the University Galleries hold their annual Awards Show each spring. The exhibition is juried by two guest jurors and features work by current art students. The Awards Show exhibition includes several award categories as well as merit awards. You can read about the requirements for each category on our Scholarships page . We encourage you to apply if you are eligible. The competition is open to undergraduate and graduate students, as well as all matriculated and Open University students, enrolled at Sac State during the current academic year.
Congrats to all of our Awards Show and Scholarship recipients! We encourage all students to apply again next year!

Each spring, the College of Arts & Letters presents a showcase of all A&L programs through a week-long celebration. The Art Department participates through workshops, visiting artists and lectures, and the Art History Symposium. Stay tuned for more details about FOTA 2026!
Find Out More About the Festival of the Arts

The Department of Art is pleased to announce that the Art History program will be offering an Italy study abroad course again in January 2026. Experience art in person in Italy and use the cities of Rome and Florence as your classroom. This nine-day program is designed to make art more accessible for participants and will focus on art of the Italian peninsula from the ancient period to the year 1800. Visits to the Colosseum, Roman Forum, Vatican, and Palazzo Medici are some of the many highlights of this program!
Read more about studying abroad in Italy!

At the end of every Spring semester, we hold a huge celebration that spans across all areas of the Art Department and all galleries on campus called Art Ball. This is a chance to honor the hard work of all our Art students at once before we leave for summer break.

links.text: Refer to the Witt Gallery Exhibition Guide
You can read about the requirements for each category on our Scholarships page
Find Out More About the Festival of the Arts

pages.title: Facilities & Safety in the Art Department | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Department of Art is an advocate for maintaining a studio environment that in the interest of preventing reasonably avoidable harm, accidental injury or illness, shall encourage the preservation of health and safety for all populations who use the studio art facilities in pursuit of higher learning and community outreach.
headings: Department of Art College of Arts & Letters
Facilities & Safety in the Art Department
contentBlocks: The Department of Art utilizes the following on-campus buildings and facilities for instruction in the studio disciplines:

Kadema Hall houses the Art Department office, faculty offices, Art Education and Art History classrooms, studios for Ceramics, Printmaking, Beginning Painting and Drawing, the New Media Art lab facilities and the Witt and Else Galleries.
Mariposa Hall 1007 houses the New Media Art computer labs.
Art Studio Lab houses technician and faculty offices, graduate student's individual studios, shops and studios for Sculpture, Painting, and Drawing.

Students currently enrolled in Art Studio classes are encouraged to get a key fob to grant access to the studio facilities and resources, as well as to keep students safe during open lab hours, especially after hours. Students may only be issued keys or fobs if their faculty has requested their access.
If approved for a key or fob by your faculty:
If you are already in enrolled in a class that has been approved for a key/fob, it will be ready to be picked up from Facilities Management in the first week of the semester.
If you enroll late in a class that has been approved for a key/fob, it will not be ready to be picked up from Facilities Management until Census.

Visit Facilities Management to pick up your key/fob, if you do not already have one. The office is located at the front of campus, along the J street side of campus off Bay Laurel Way, behind the Athletics Center.
Return the key/fob to Facilities Management at the end of the semester/year.

links.text: Facilities & Safety in the Art Department
